steps.title: Identify the greatest common factor
steps.content: The original expression is $7x^6 - 2x^5 + 9x^4$. The powers of $x$ in each term are \$6$, \$5$, and \$4$ respectively. The lowest power is \$4$, so the GCF is $x^4$.

steps.title: Factor out the GCF and match the choice
steps.content:
Divide each term by $x^4$:
$$7x^6 \div x^4 = 7x^2$$
$$-2x^5 \div x^4 = -2x$$
$$9x^4 \div x^4 = 9$$

Therefore:
$$7x^6 - 2x^5 + 9x^4 = x^4(7x^2 - 2x + 9)$$
That matches choice B — B is correct.
